tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria with glycosuria and mild ketonuria, but no hematuria or leukocyturia. Urine is cloudy, brown, and has low specific gravity. These findings suggest impaired renal function, possibly acute or chronic kidney disease, with evidence of tubular dysfunction (glycosuria, proteinuria) but no clear signs of urinary tract infection. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
